title: eustachys neglecta
intro: Eustachys neglecta, also known as the Desert Lavender, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas and the Caribbean. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
